gm_output.player_facing_text: Rain spatters against the soot-dark stones of Cinderwatch's eastern gate, the chill of the downpour mixing with the earthy scent of wet mud beneath your boots. Above, frayed banners hang limply, their colors faded by time and storm. The narrow approach is clogged with a ragged procession of refugees and creaking wagons, their weariness etched in every slow step, while guards stand rigid, holding the passage with a fragile discipline that hints at the tensions waiting to snap. Nearby, a well-appointed townhouse keeps vigilant watch over the muddy square, its noble colors a sharp contrast to the grime below. A figure dressed in threadbare clothes stands motionless at the edge of the crowd, eyes flickering more to the travelers' packs and faces than to the gate itself. From the corner of your eye, a tavern runner calls out urgently through the rain, hawking hot stew and whispered rumors for a few copper coins. Close at hand, a wooden notice board leans under the weight of new decrees—tax increases, curfew mandates, and most strikingly, a posted warning about a patrol gone missing somewhere beyond the town. Two alleys diverge from the muddy approach; one is narrow and swallowed by shadow, the other wider, leading toward the flickering lanterns of the tavern district. Crates mired in mud and strewn with wet straw crowd one edge, their sharp scent mingling with the chatter and footfalls as the crowd swells. The atmosphere tightens imperceptibly, a current of unease threading through the clamorous waiting throng around the gate.

opening_selector_selected_facts: Rain spatters soot-dark stone; frayed banners hang above Cinderwatch's eastern gate.
A well-appointed townhouse overlooking the square flies noble colors above the mud and clamor.
One figure in threadbare clothes stands uncannily still, gaze flicking to packs and faces more than the gate queue.
Refugees, wagons, and foot traffic clog the muddy approach while guards hold the choke.
A tavern runner shouts offers of hot stew and paid rumor.
A notice board lists new taxes, curfews, and a posted warning about a missing patrol.

rule_priority_order: ANSWER THE PLAYER
DO NOT CONTRADICT AUTHORITATIVE STATE
DO NOT LEAK HIDDEN FACTS / SECRETS
DO NOT ASSERT UNRESOLVED OUTCOMES, HIDDEN TRUTHS, OR NPC INTENT AS SETTLED FACT
PRESERVE PLAYER AGENCY — DO NOT DECIDE ACTIONS OR UPGRADE SURFACED LEADS INTO MANDATORY PATHS
IF FULL CERTAINTY IS UNAVAILABLE, GIVE A BOUNDED PARTIAL ANSWER
WHEN THE PLAYER PRESSES THE SAME TOPIC AGAIN, ADD NET-NEW VALUE RATHER THAN RESTATING
KEEP NARRATION AND SPOKEN LINES DISTINCT; PREFER NEW SIGNAL OVER GENERIC FILLER; RESPECT FALLBACK BREVITY
MAINTAIN DIEGETIC VOICE (no validator/system voice)
KEEP NARRATION FREE OF INTERNAL SCAFFOLDS, MENU LABELS, AND ENGINE COACHING
PRESERVE SCENE MOMENTUM
ADD SPECIFICITY / FLAVOR / POLISH

no_validator_voice.rule: Never speak as a validator, analyst, referee of canon, or system. Do not mention what is or is not established, available to the model, visible to tools, or answerable by the system. If uncertainty exists, express it as in-world uncertainty from people, circumstances, clues, distance, darkness, rumor, missing access, or incomplete observation.

preferred_deescalations: Tighten diction and body language without 'or else' phrasing.
Use deadlines, policy, or reputation risk as leverage.
